name: Colognian (Kölsch)

sources.author: Wrede, Adam
sources.title: Neuer Kölnischer Sprachschatz
sources.notes: Standard Kölsch dictionary; a starting point for a future orthography map.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Colognian phonology
sources.notes: Full phoneme inventory (consonants, long/short vowels, diphthongs). States that spelling can follow several standards and gives no orthography table.

sources.author: Bhatt, Christa
sources.title: Kölsche Schreibregeln: Vorschläge für eine Rechtschreibung des Kölschen
sources.publisher: Akademie för uns kölsche Sproch / Greven Verlag, Köln
sources.notes: The only candidate written norm (explicitly 'proposals'). Print-only; not consulted, so no grapheme map is derived from it.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Rheinische Dokumenta
sources.notes: A phonetic transcription system for Rhinelandic dialects with a full sign->IPA table — NOT the orthography Kölsch is written in, hence not adopted here.

notes: STUB (deliberate): the grapheme inventory of Kölsch is NOT specified. Kölsch (Ripoarisch) is the Ripuarian (Central Franconian) dialect of Cologne. There is no fetchable, citable letter->IPA norm: the Wikipedia article on Colognian phonology states that 'spelling of Colognian can follow several standards' and gives IPA only, with no orthography table. The Akademie för uns kölsche Sproch's rules exist only in print (Christa Bhatt, 'Kölsche Schreibregeln: Vorschläge für eine Rechtschreibung des Kölschen' — 'Vorschläge' = proposals, not a norm) and could not be consulted; the Rheinische Dokumenta is a phonetic notation system, not the orthography Kölsch is actually written in. Kölsch also carries a phonemic tone accent (Rhenish Accent 1 / Accent 2) that no spelling marks. The phoneme inventory IS documented (consonants incl. /ɕ ɧ ʝ ʁ/; a large long/short vowel system; diphthongs /aɪ aːʊ aʊ eɪ iɐ̯ oʊ ɔʏ øʏ/), but an inventory is not a grapheme map. INPUT CONTRACT (for any future spec): Latin-script, German-convention Kölsch text, after ONE written norm is obtained and cited.

ancestors.notes: Germanic — the classification node this language sits under.
